findings.issue: WebSocket and restore gateway adapters used fabricated live bindings and constant replay state.
Caller-provided metadata bypassed gateway sanitization before writeInput.
Gateway tests did not cover WsRouter/restore integration risk.
Submit intent without newline was accepted without mcp:message.submit scope.

checks: Gateway denies replay before policy fallback and target write.
WebSocket and restore ingress delegate to SessionInputGateway while preserving SessionManager.writeInput as low-level PTY writer.
WebSocket gateway reads actual client replay and screen-repair pending state.
Protected MCP/open-agent submit intent requires mcp:message.submit scope even without CR/LF bytes.
Secret-bearing webhook and caller metadata are sanitized before audit and write metadata.
